skill.name: gdpr-dsgvo-expert
skill.description: Senior GDPR/DSGVO expert and internal/external auditor for data protection compliance. Provides EU GDPR and German DSGVO expertise, privacy impact assessments, data protection auditing, and compliance verification. Use for GDPR compliance assessments, privacy audits, data protection planning, and regulatory compliance verification.
skill.summary: Senior GDPR/DSGVO expert and internal/external auditor for data protection compliance. Provides EU G...
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, external command, environment, and network reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives caused by GDPR terminology, Markdown code fences, inline resource filenames, and placeholder documentation, with no evidence of malicious intent or executable risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits are documentation text, GDPR terminology, or placeholder examples. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashing routines, encryption configuration, or unsafe algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain legal, compliance, or placeholder prose rather than code. Review found no weak cryptographic algorithm usage in these files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns are Markdown formatting and inline file names. They do not instruct the assistant to execute commands and do not contain shell payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited lines are Markdown fences or literal resource names. There is no executable Ruby, shell command, command substitution, or user input path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment And Network Terms
low_findings.description: The environment access and network reconnaissance hits are benign compliance text. The reviewed lines discuss criminal conviction data, organizational measures, and privacy risk metrics, not environment variables or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text has no process environment access, socket use, scanner command, host enumeration, or outbound request. It is part of a legal compliance checklist.

content.value_statement: Privacy teams need structured help to assess GDPR and German DSGVO obligations. This skill provides audit frameworks, DPIA guidance, rights workflows, and compliance planning support.
actual_capabilities: Maps GDPR and DSGVO compliance work into governance, legal basis, rights, accountability, and transfer areas.
Guides Data Protection Impact Assessment planning, execution, documentation, and review.
Structures data subject rights intake, verification, fulfillment, response, and documentation workflows.
Supports German privacy compliance topics, including BDSG coordination and supervisory authority considerations.
Provides audit planning, evidence review, remediation planning, and DPO support frameworks.
Highlights international transfer assessment steps and supplementary safeguard considerations.
limitations: It does not replace legal advice from qualified counsel or a Data Protection Officer.
The included reference and asset files are placeholders, not complete legal templates.
The included script is a placeholder and does not automate GDPR checks.
It does not provide real-time regulatory updates or jurisdiction-specific legal conclusions.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Internal GDPR Audit
use_cases.description: Create a scoped audit plan, evidence request list, and finding structure for a privacy program review.

use_cases.title: Plan a DPIA for High-Risk Processing
use_cases.description: Build a DPIA workflow that covers necessity, proportionality, risk assessment, mitigation, and DPO review.

use_cases.title: Improve Rights Request Operations
use_cases.description: Design a repeatable process for access, erasure, rectification, portability, objection, and restriction requests.

prompt_templates.title: Identify GDPR Obligations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this processing activity and list the main GDPR or DSGVO obligations. Include legal basis, data subject rights, documentation, and risk areas.

prompt_templates.title: Create a DPIA Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a DPIA for this product. Cover threshold assessment, processing description, necessity, proportionality, risk analysis, mitigations, and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Privacy Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an internal GDPR audit plan for this organization. Include scope, evidence requests, interview topics, testing steps, and report sections.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Cross-Border Transfers
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this international data transfer scenario. Identify transfer mechanisms, risk assessment needs, supplementary measures, documentation, and escalation points.

output_examples.input: We process customer support tickets from EU users in a US-hosted support platform.
output_examples.output: The output would identify the processing purpose, personal data categories, processor relationship, transfer mechanism, data subject rights impacts, and documentation tasks.

output_examples.input: We are launching a connected medical device that collects patient telemetry.
output_examples.output: The output would outline a DPIA plan, special category data concerns, lawful basis questions, technical safeguards, stakeholder reviews, and residual risk handling.

output_examples.input: We need to prepare for a data protection authority inquiry.
output_examples.output: The output would organize evidence, define response roles, list key records, prepare interview topics, and identify legal counsel coordination needs.

best_practices: Provide the processing purpose, data categories, systems, recipients, retention periods, and transfer locations.
Ask for structured outputs such as audit plans, DPIA sections, evidence lists, or remediation trackers.
Have legal counsel or a qualified DPO review final compliance decisions before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill output as formal legal advice or a final regulatory position.
Do not omit processing context and expect a reliable GDPR assessment.
Do not use placeholder references or assets as production-ready compliance documentation.

faq.question: Can this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured compliance support, but qualified counsel or a DPO should approve legal conclusions.

faq.question: Does it cover both GDPR and DSGVO?
faq.answer: Yes. It addresses EU GDPR concepts and German DSGVO implementation topics, including BDSG coordination.

faq.question: Can it create a DPIA?
faq.answer: It can guide DPIA planning and drafting. A responsible privacy professional should validate the final DPIA.

faq.question: Does it automate compliance checks?
faq.answer: No. The current script is only a placeholder and does not perform automated checks.

faq.question: Can it help with data subject rights requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure request intake, identity verification, fulfillment steps, response timelines, and documentation.

faq.question: Is it suitable for international transfer reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize transfer mechanism assessment, risk review, supplementary measures, and documentation needs.
